title: The Reverie Opens Under Hidden Shadows
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Reverie Delegates, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7, Garp, Unknown person #8 as The Reverie gathers world leaders while hidden powers at Mariejois begin to move.
scene: World leaders gather at Mariejois while the Revolutionary Army and the secret ruler behind the Empty Throne move in the shadows.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So all of these people are Royals and their body- guards?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Sam- Bo!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: From all over the world?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Mam- Bo!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: From tomorrow,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: When the seven days of the conference start, I doubt even your relatives will have time to be bored.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: The food and drink are quite luxurious.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: WEAPONS HAVE BEEN CONFISCATED, OF COURSE, BUT...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: These bodyguards sure do look strong!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and father seems to be getting along well.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: That's a relief.

dialogue.speaker: Reverie Delegates
dialogue.text: My, my! A seafood Princess!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Your breath- taking beauty belies even the rumors of its magnificence. (1)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: My son, you see, is single. How about it?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: PAPA! This one ain't bad. She ain't bad.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oi, don't rush that sale! I've got a son, too, y'kno.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: P- PAPPY! I like this girl!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No, no! Mermaid Princess, please, my son...:)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: OHOHOHO! I've got three fine princes in my house!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: AH... um...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: You're NOT MY TYPE!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Shirahoo- SHIII!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oh, dear. What my sister just said is actually a common phrase in our dialect, which really means "I've not yet married either," and...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In this sort of situation, you've got to say things like "Oh, I'm so flattered!" or "let us go for food sometime, then!" to mollify them! Even if they're lies!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: How dare you! You've shamed me!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Whaaaaa?! I- I cant imagine doing something like lying...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you needlessly insult them, we'll have an international incident on our hands-famiredo! Let's go over there.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's just to get them off your tail! It's not really a lie.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: WAAH!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: So cute!:)

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: KJEH!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's not a very nice thing to say to a warrior!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Are you okay, Leo?!

dialogue.speaker: Reverie Delegates
dialogue.text: lady-in-waiting of the Dressrosa kingdom

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm so sorry! I didn't mean to offend you.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: WHATA-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Then it's okay!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We only revealed ourselves because you had said you know Luffy- Land.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: So cute...

dialogue.speaker: Garp
dialogue.text: We're Luffyland's henchmen! We're pirates, you see!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I never thought that I'd be able to talk with so many people about Lucy at Reverie.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But I guess him and his crew really have traveled the world.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Yes. They don't want to become heroes,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So their boundaries and rumors follow them around, only worsening their reputation, but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: I'm sure they haven't changed at all!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Tidbits about the Alabasta Kingdom

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: VIVI, Princess of a desert kingdom on the Grand Line, once sailed with the straw hats for a time as a Navigator.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A rebellion broke out in Vivi's home- land, and behind it lurked the former shichibukai cro- codile and his evil machinations. Luffy and his friends stopped him and saved the country.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: However, the govern-ment reported these deeds as achievements of the Marines.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You know, I had a thought when I read the article on what happened in dressrossa.

dialogue.speaker: Garp
dialogue.text: luffy and the others...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They probably decided to save someone else, huh, I thought.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Yes, they did!

summary: This section advances the Reverie storyline with 54 dialogue beats. The Reverie looks ceremonial on the surface, but Imu, the Five Elders, and the Revolutionaries turn it into a major political pivot.
